title: Cheilanthes pohliana
taxonomy: Cheilanthes pohliana is classified within the family Aspleniaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair fern family. This species belongs to the genus Cheilanthes, which comprises a group of ferns often referred to as the lip ferns. The classification of Cheilanthes pohliana is further supported by its unique characteristics, including its frond shape and leaf venation pattern. Cheilanthes pohliana is native to the Americas, specifically found in the southwestern region of the United States and parts of Mexico. The species is often grown in gardens and is prized for its attractive, delicate fronds.

intro: Cheilanthes pohliana, also known as the Polynesian lip fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Aspleniaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cheilanthes pohliana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
